summary.rationale: Positive employee sentiment; moderate innovation; 40 reviews

reviews.title: One of the best retail jobs for growth and culture!
reviews.pros: The team culture is incredibly supportive, diverse, and encouraging. Management is accessible and genuinely invested in employee development, which makes a big difference day-to-day. The work environment is positive and collaborative. The benefits are among the best you’ll find in retail. It’s a great place to build communication, problem-solving, and customer experience skills.
reviews.cons: At times the store can become hectic, especially when there's high customer demand and not enough staff on the floor. This makes multitasking and attention to detail crucial, which can feel overwhelming during peak hours. Like any retail role, you’ll also occasionally encounter difficult customers, which requires patience and strong interpersonal skills.
reviews.advice: Continue reinforcing training around tools and internal processes, especially for newer employees. While training is strong overall, some practical learnings can get overlooked and are often picked up on the job. Ensuring consistency here could make the transition smoother and improve efficiency on the floor.

reviews.title: Challenging, Rewarding,  but ZERO  work/life balance.   Most satisfying ,   most difficult and most  rewarding job ever.
reviews.pros: We work with geniuses - in every department,   We create innovative products that thrill our customers and create new product categories -  who else can say that?
reviews.cons: ZERO   ZERO   ZERO   work/life balance.   Execs have been saying for YEARS that they understand and will make it better.  But in actuality,  it gets worse every year!  It is obviously top management LIP SERVICE because if they meant it,  they could fix it tomorrow.   They have hundreds of BILLIONS in the bank.   If they REALLY cared about employee work/life balance,  they could bring aboard the right number of folks to make that issue dissolve.   Sick of hearing the lies.   Just don't lie about wanting to fix it,  when they clearly DON'T care

reviews.title: At-Home-Advisor
reviews.pros: The position is at home. The company is AMAZING. There are limitless advancement opportunities. You work with some very cool people and the leadership cares about your development. You may get coaching but you never get battered or belittled. The pay is decent and the benefits include, 401(k) match, stock purchase options, product discounts and discounts on services across many different areas, education assistance, child care assistance, paid vacation, sick time and other time off options, health club Reimbursment or bike cost set off. You get 1.5 time for OT and it's pretty much unlimited as long as you don't exceed 12 hours in a day or 59 total in a week.
reviews.cons: The schedule changes every 90 days and you have to bid for your shifts so you never know what you are going to get. This makes it extremely hard to plan your life to far in advance. You are also pretty much assured to work a Saturday or Sunday.
reviews.advice: The management consists of a team manager and their area manager. Meetings are held over video and chat. They are supportive of individual development and help you to reach your goal. Management will challenge you if you want to advance but it's always in the spirit of making you better to prepare you for the next role.

reviews.title: Changing the world (in a small way)
reviews.pros: Working at Apple means that the things you work on get into the hands of hundreds of millions of people, literally. It's a lot of pressure to get things right and drives one to do the best work of their lives. 2+ years later I'm still blown away by the talent and creativity of people I work with.
reviews.cons: It's a lot of work, teams are usually strapped for resources and under pressure to deliver on strict timelines leading to high stress and long hours at certain times during the year.
reviews.advice: Keep fighting for "what's right". Renewable energy, civil liberties, working condition, everything. I couldn't be prouder of that aspect of the company, in the end it's about a lot more than profits and earnings.

reviews.title: A Company as Meticulous as Their Products!
reviews.pros: Competitive Pay, Great Benefits, Amazing People, Great Work, Awesome Opportunities, Excellent Experiences, Great atmospheres in a beautiful store, no matter where you're working!
reviews.cons: None! I had one of the best times ever at this company. Hopefully I stay to go on to corporate!
reviews.advice: You guys are awesome. Keep up the good work!

reviews.title: Least Evil of the FAANG companies
reviews.pros: After about 6 months in contract, it is clear that Apple, for the most part, is making a good-faith effort to treat me and my coworkers well.
reviews.cons: My lead is a "superstar". A pleasure to work with and genuinely brilliant. The down-side is that they're veeeeery busy. I do not sense that Apple intended this as a "throw 'em in and see if they swim" position. But because of my lead's limited availability there's been a lot that I've had to figure out myself. At least one of my coworkers had a similar experience, being with Apple almost a year before getting properly ramped up to speed.
reviews.advice: Keep treating your employees well. Apple does need to be better with on-boarding new employees. Because so much of the source code is internal, we can't figure stuff out by just googling it. Do not rush into the AI bandwagon (so far you haven't). Apple is better off making an informed decision on it's AI strategy AFTER the AI bubble has popped. (...and it WILL pop.)

reviews.title: Started out great, became very unpleasant and even hostile in the end
reviews.pros: Competitive pay and benefits, product discounts
reviews.cons: Very little opportunity for advancement, Nonstop call volume, rude customers, micromanagement down to policing bathroom breaks and threatening to fire you for taking them, Management targets people who use FMLA, ever expanding scope of support, removal of worker friendly benefits such as the option to take unpaid time off.
reviews.advice: Come together and take a stand against leadership. Advocate for your employees instead of bending to leadership and enforcing inhumane and undignified policies. Remember that when AHA goes away, your jobs do as well.

reviews.title: Good job, but extremely stressful
reviews.pros: Apple does really focus on a work life balance and allows you to take a lot of time to yourself if needed (and approved).
reviews.cons: Complete and total burnout is expected. Data Center logistics can be good, but expect a LOT to be put on your plate. I cannot count the amount of projects that we (or just me) would be handed and told next to nothing about how to accomplish it. It was an extremely, "sink or swim" place that, I think, THEY thought made you stronger, but in reality it just makes you question why you're going at 150% at all times for the salary you are making.
reviews.advice: They need to hire more people and stop thinking that their small teams can handle the extreme loads they already have.

reviews.title: Good place to start for recent graduate from high school or college
reviews.pros: Co is a good place to learn customer service skills and receive exposure to technical products.
reviews.cons: 15 years ago I read a review of Apple as an employer. The review stated the pros I listed in the previous box. This same review listed at that time 15 years ago that it was difficult to advance career wise. I still think this is true today. While I was working for Apple, it appeared to me that the company didn’t make long-term investments in its employees. This is a good place to start a career; however if you want to advance in a technical career, work for another company or get a technical degree at a university.
reviews.job_title: Product Zone Specialist/At Home Technical Advisor

reviews.title: Great learning experience and supportive management
reviews.pros: Got to learn a lot and the benefits were amazing
reviews.cons: Survey scores were graded regularly and you had to have at least a 90+% CSAT score you’d have a meeting with your supervisor but my supervisor was amazing and always gave positive feedback and wanted to help.
reviews.advice: Please be positive and understanding with your workers.

reviews.title: Great Working Experience (Seasonal Worker)
reviews.pros:
• Very Supportive Environment
• Very Inclusive (Race, Gender, LGBTQ, Differently Abled, Immigrant, Clean Skin/Piercings/Tattoos, etc.)
• Management & Leadership are ALWAYS willing to help
• High Standard environment
• Good Employment Bonus
• Often have FREE Snacks available in their lunch room
reviews.cons: Apple Retail Specialists (Sales Staff) receive incredibly low pay for the expert care and top-quality service they provide.
reviews.advice: Sales staff are the backbone of your retail business and so they should be paid at least $28/hr. ESPECIALLY when expected to perform at the extremely high standard that Apple demands.

reviews.title: Strange Team but really great experience
reviews.pros: Great Pay and networking opportunities.
reviews.cons: Lack of direction for my project. My iBuddy was Out of office 70% of the time so I lacked actual guidance and had to seek external help which wasn't the easiest due the secrecy and privacy guidelines. Only a certain number of people are aware of certain ongoing projects, and you cant share info with any other person outside the knowledge group.

reviews.title: Great opportunity
reviews.pros: Great team, product, and nice to have one of the top tech companies on your resume.
reviews.cons: Work life balance and in-office requirements can be taxing because of the expectations that come with working for Apple.

reviews.title: Great Company, Wrong Manager
reviews.pros:
-great benefits
-good location
-hybrid work schedule
-great pay, not too difficult
-tons of career paths
reviews.cons:
-if you get the wrong manager & they don’t see you as bubbly and extra friendly, they’ll make your life miserable and invent issues that don’t exist
-not a ton of diversity in the workforce
-for a company worth billions, raises were still under 5% annually, no bonuses for the team I was on, and the perks (discounts, 3rd party & in-house, were fewer and fewer) and the stock gifting was totally dependent on your manager

reviews.title: Great experience, but not what it used to be
reviews.pros:
Incredible opportunity to work on the world's best products and most of the people really care about the product. It was a lot of fun to see how the world's most admired company works behind the scenes. If you have a good manager, you can go pretty far, pretty quick.

Compensation and PTO is very fair for Silicon Valley. The culture is very design and user experience driven which may seem obvious, but you should feel the same way before joining. Sometimes sacrifices in the technical implementation are made because it provides a better user design.
reviews.cons:
My main complaint is that it gets repetitive after a few years, specifically working on the OS side, you just feel like it's the same cycle-driven work year in and year out. Work life balance can be tough, especially leading up to major OS releases.

The hybrid schedule is nice, but the in office days force you to commute to/from Cupertino to sit in online meetings. I put a low score for DEI because there is not a huge representation of underserved minorities in tech. That becomes apparent from day one on campus, despite what the values are of the company.
reviews.advice:
The obvious thing here is to stop making your engineers who have successful track records come into the office. It is a waste of time and the old Apple "water cooler" culture is dead. Over the years, I have seen some exceptional engineers and people walk out due to this aging policy that has a tremendous influence on the industry.

Not a lot of leadership from underrepresented (in-tech) groups which is discouraging given the public perception of the company in the marketing.

reviews.title: Unreliable contract
reviews.pros:
- fast interview
- some nice colleagues
reviews.cons:
- main: contract instability (can be fired after 1 month due to poor budgeting) + 1.5 year policy after which contract ends
- short and unclear on-boarding, no clear documentation, no help from manager
- survive or sink culture
- limitations for contractors and no recognition (your achievements will be assigned to employee)

reviews.title: Great Benefits, Good place to work depending on dept
reviews.pros: Apple is still an incredible company with world-class talent, strong benefits, and the opportunity to work on products and systems that operate at massive global scale. The people on the ground—engineers, operators, program managers—are deeply committed, smart, and mission-driven. When teams are empowered, the impact can be incredibly rewarding.
reviews.cons:
Within IS&T the recent wave of reorganizations has been deeply discouraging. Roles and responsibilities have shifted frequently, often without clear strategy or long-term vision. Strong contributors have been moved into positions they did not want or were never designed for, while leadership promotions increasingly appear disconnected from operational experience or proven impact.
There is also a growing sense of cultural narrowing at the leadership level. Decision-making and advancement seem to favor a very specific leadership profile and way of thinking, which limits diversity of perspective and can stifle healthy debate and innovation. As a result, many experienced employees feel stuck, undervalued, or uncertain about their future despite years of strong performance.
reviews.advice: Stability matters. Invest more in transparent career paths, reward demonstrated results over optics, and broaden what “good leadership” looks like. Apple succeeds when it values diverse viewpoints and empowers people closest to the work. Re-orgs should enable teams—not exhaust them.

reviews.title: Managing risk in a factory
reviews.pros: Things gets done and on time .
reviews.cons: No innovation, extreme top down culture, people just please management. Lots of empty work and hyper polished keynotes.
reviews.advice: Get real, times are changing fast.

reviews.title: Good expierence
reviews.pros: Good project, helpful manager but he was always busy
reviews.cons: throw you into deep end. Not always much support

reviews.title: pretty great
reviews.pros: very nice campus, good views
reviews.cons: long hours, hard work, tough boss

reviews.title: Lots of Pros and Lots of Cons
reviews.pros: Pay was generous for a part time retail job. Worked with some incredible people. Often felt like I was doing Good.
reviews.cons: Acted like we were all a family, but then pushed us harder and harder each quarter as we had to show improvement, even as some people were far above expectations. Rules constantly changing, the best way to navigate the day was to know who to ask your question to so that the answer would be what you need done. A crazy number of managers competed to the number of regular staff members. "I'll look into that" with no follow up was a common occurrence.

reviews.title: They keep overworking you
reviews.pros: The benefits are A+. From the healthcare to the free services.
reviews.cons:
Very metrics driven. Be expected to receive conversations for not hitting numerous metrics 

Almost impossible to move up

reviews.title: Great company.
reviews.pros: Apple took care of everything, especially in COVID times.  They were great with all aspects of life
reviews.cons: I do not have any cons
reviews.advice: I do not have any advice for management.

reviews.title: Fun workplace
reviews.pros:
Easy work, as long as customers are nice.
Employees are usually very helpful when you are starting out. Lots of helpful training.
reviews.cons: No real career path unless you have 10 years of time to wait for a management position, maybe longer
reviews.advice: Make it easier to move up, and base it off performance, not seniority.

reviews.title: Great but demanding
reviews.pros:
- Interesting & diverse coworkers
- Very cool area to work in. 
- Competitive pay (for retail)
- Great stepping stone for your next job (Apple on the resume never hurts)
- Awesome perks (if you graduate from seasonal employee to part-time)
- You learn to be an extrovert very fast
- Interviewing skills for life
- Sweet training experience
- Great support with whatever you need honestly
reviews.cons:
- It's still retail. 
- Insufferable customers + hovering managers = pain
- TRAFFIC AND ROAD CLOSURES ALL THE TIME
- Pay for parking $$$$
- Information overload 
- Company and CEO are questionable
- Difficult work life balance especially if you're a student
- Communication with management is a maze
- I can't overstate just how spoiled these customers are. If you're not used to being around this tax bracket: brace yourself.
reviews.advice: Don't be fake. Say what you really feel and cut the faux smiles.

reviews.title: Could be 1 star or 5, no in between
reviews.pros: Brand reputation, stability, works like a start-up despite being on of the biggest tech company
reviews.cons:
Terrible employee benefits and company morale when it comes to employee well-being. This is a company that you will like buying its stock but not working for it.

Bad tech for SWEs, inexperienced managers, almost non-exist web for teams under hardware, culture can get toxic and you may be thrown under the bus if you don't have good mentors or managers.
reviews.advice: Invest in company benefits, gather anonymous employee feedback to improve things, build more trust with stake holders, not everything has to be secretive if there isn't a need.

reviews.title: amazing
reviews.pros: nice company lot of money
reviews.cons: too much hectic workload wouldnt recommend

reviews.title: Great benefits but horrible micromanaging
reviews.pros: Amazing benefits , really good discounts.
reviews.cons: Constant micromanaging from all sides . Hours are bad . It’s hard to have a work life balance. They don’t have alot of room to move up with the company
reviews.advice: Start caring about your employees needs instead of trying to boost your bonus checks

reviews.title: awesome
reviews.pros:
Pros
Brand prestige: Working at Apple instantly boosts your résumé. It opens doors in tech, marketing, design, and even unrelated industries.

Culture of excellence: You’re surrounded by sharp, detail-obsessed people. The standards are sky-high, which can push you to grow faster than anywhere else.

Product ecosystem perks: Deep discounts on Apple products and early exposure to new tech. Employees genuinely get excited about launches.

Strong benefits: Excellent healthcare, stock purchase plan, and paid time off. Full-time employees often feel financially secure.

Training and structure: Whether you’re in a store or corporate, training is thorough. Apple’s onboarding and communication systems are elite.

Diversity and inclusion (mostly real): Company-wide emphasis on diversity, though it varies by location or team.

Cool factor: People light up when you say you work at Apple. There’s real social capital attached to the brand.
reviews.cons:
Cons
Workload and intensity: The “Apple perfectionism” culture means you’ll work long hours or feel like you should. Burnout is common, especially in retail and engineering.

Micromanagement: Leadership can be rigid. There’s a “this is how Apple does it” mindset that leaves little room for personal style or flexibility.

Internal secrecy: You often can’t discuss projects, even with coworkers. Communication silos make collaboration weirdly difficult for such a huge company.

Limited upward mobility (retail): It’s hard to move from retail to corporate, no matter how talented you are. Many employees feel stuck unless they leave and reapply elsewhere.

Culture of fear: Mistakes are magnified. Some managers use pressure instead of mentorship. It’s not a “safe to fail” environment.

Corporate politics: Promotions and recognition sometimes depend on who you know, not just what you produce.

Retail schedules: If you’re in-store, expect constant weekends and holiday shifts. “Work-life balance” depends entirely on your manager.

reviews.title: Retail
reviews.pros: The people (coworkers) are the best people you will meet. Lifelong friendships! Best job for retail experience.
reviews.cons: Helping 3-4 customers an hour and forced to get Business introductions as well. Metrics is all they are concerned with now.and helping someone new every 15-20 minutes can be draining.

reviews.title: Great atmosphere
reviews.pros: Innovative Culture: Working at Apple means being at the forefront of design and technology. The company’s commitment to innovation pushes designers to think creatively and solve complex problems.
reviews.cons: Competitive Environment: The high caliber of talent at Apple means the environment can be competitive, which might not suit everyone.

reviews.title: Place to work
reviews.pros: Amazing place to work if you really want to work. You are treated like a human being.
reviews.cons: Difficult to get a full time position.

reviews.title: Front Software Developer
reviews.pros:
Compensation
Work/life balance
Development opportunities
reviews.cons: Intense and stressful at moments

reviews.title: Amazing company, Yet to decide
reviews.pros: Great reach to the world
reviews.cons: not bad, could be better management

reviews.title: Good benefits but not worth the pressure
reviews.pros: Access to several development tools and health benefits.
reviews.cons: Mostly lateral moves with little to no pay raise. Unrealistic goals and a constant demand to embrace tasks that were once done by other people/groups.
reviews.advice: Please remember you are dealing with humans not AI.

reviews.title: Great College Job, terrible internal promotions
reviews.pros: Great benefits as part time, espp, loved my team that I worked with at Micigan Ave
reviews.cons: Scheduling, favoritism amongst leadership team - seen a lot with promotions and who gets fired vs a warning, heavy on Apple Care conversions or business intros - hard when we're selling to international customers where they might not have AC+ support, very much lack of ability to move upward unless you drown yourself in Apple (work, sacrifice your time for them), some leaders do not lead by example (but they've been fired - just took wayyyy too long)
reviews.advice: Quit the favoritism, and treat the hard working employees well who deserve the promotions and tell the difference if someone is doing well or just trying to suck up to you. This isn't corporate sales - relax a bit.

reviews.title: It was great until it wasn't
reviews.pros: Great to get your feet wet in "tech" very retail based now. The relationships I was able to foster at Apple will last a life time. I had to privilege to make a lot of great friends. I also was able to develop key skills that will help me excel in an enterprise environment. It also forced me to communicate with a wide range of people which helped with my communication skills greatly,
reviews.cons:
No work life balance. I worked every weekend after I became a genius making it feel impossible to see any of my family or friends.
The managers were quite poor with bad decision making skills and showed clear favoritism.
They moved my old Store Manager to a different store after word spread around that we were planing on unionizing. The new Store Manager was the worst person I've worked for. Extremely cut throat in every way possible. He would smile in your face and tell you how great of a job your doing turn around and fire you the next hour about some random "policy" violation. 
I was skipped over for multiple promotions even after showing that I was willing and ready.

Leaving this job was the best decision I've ever made. I would never work at another fruit stand again.

reviews.title: Fun products rewarding work
reviews.pros:
Good job security I would say is one of the biggest pros
Apple Park is beautiful
Fun products
reviews.cons: Pay is a little low compared to other big tech companies

reviews.title: Cliquey, bad leadership
reviews.pros: Half decent retail experience relative to other retail companies
reviews.cons: Terrible, cliquey environment, bad leadership, creepy tactics.

reviews.title: N/A
reviews.pros:
Good work life balance
Collaborative team mebers
reviews.cons:
Standard benefits
Small office
No free products
